title: Gingerly Dense Korean Sauerkraut Patbingsu
description: A hearty, comforting dessert featuring sauerkraut, sweet potato, and creamy ice cream, inspired by the Korean patbingsu form.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C). Pierce the sweet potatoes with a fork a few times to allow steam to escape.
Bake the sweet potatoes for 45-60 minutes, or until they are tender when pierced with a fork.
While the sweet potatoes are baking, toast the sesame seeds in a small pan over medium heat, stirring frequently, until fragrant and lightly browned.
In a separate pan, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the grated ginger and cook for 1-2 minutes, or until fragrant.
Add the sauerkraut to the pan and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until heated through and slightly caramelized.
Stir in the honey, brown sugar, soy sauce, and black pepper. Cook for an additional minute.
To assemble the dessert, slice the baked sweet potatoes into 1-inch thick rounds.
Place a scoop of ice cream on top of each sweet potato round, followed by a spoonful of the sauerkraut-ginger mixture.
Sprinkle toasted sesame seeds, crushed peanuts, and a drizzle of sesame oil over the top.
